This commit fixes a logic error that cropped up if the baseline branch is something other than the default branch (usually `master`). Essentially, if the baseline  branch was out of date (common), then the resulting PR would have all of the commits from `master` which are missing, because it always created the new branch off of `master`, even if `master` is not the target.

pr_title: Add check for matching holder_type when inheriting
pr_body:
One frequently encountered source of errors are mismatching holder types between bases and derived classes. If either the base has `unique_ptr` holder type and the derived class `shared_ptr` (or vice versa) a segfault results.

Tracking holder types for all classes in a large codebase can be cumbersome and error prone.

This pull request is an example of how import-time checks for matching holder types can be added. I welcome your comments.

This PR adds support for `std::string_view` (and related, e.g. `std::u16string_view`) by making the current `std::string` caster slightly more generic so that it can support both `std::string`s and `std::string_view`s.

It also includes commits that update the `strings.rst` documentation with style fixes (wrapping, trailing whitespace removal) to the strings.rst documentation, and one minor wording fix.  (They don't really have anything to do with the PR aside from me noticing them when writing it).

Fixes #905 

I've added the `<string_view>` include for this directly in `cast.h` rather than push it into `stl.h`: both stdlibc++ and libc++ already `#include <string_view>` as an implementation detail of `#include <string>` when compiling in C++17 mode, so the extra header isn't actually pulling in more STL code in practice.

pr_title: Pin packaging tools that have dropped support
pr_body:
Some of the versions of Python we support are so old that even core Python packaging tools have dropped support. This causes problems like the ones seen at https://travis-ci.org/jsha/certbot/jobs/308723670#L5811. (The reason we haven't seen failures on our repo yet is because of our pip cache saved between runs.)

We're actively working on dropping support for these two versions of Python ourselves, but in the meantime, this pins these packages to the latest version that still supports the platform.

Here's the documentation for [setuptools](https://setuptools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/history.html#v37-0-0) and [wheel](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/wheel) showing that they have dropped support.
Fixes: #4484

Don't record event log on driver.
pr_body:
This removes a memory leak on the driver. Basically, every time the driver did some operation (e.g., submit a task), the timestamp was recorded in a list. That list just grew and grew and was never flushed. This PR gets rid of that list (for drivers, but not for workers).

You can see the issue by watching top and running

```python
import ray

ray.worker._init(start_ray_local=True, object_store_memory=100000000)

@ray.remote
def f():
    return 1

i = 0
while True:
    print(i)
    i += 1
    ray.get([f.remote() for _ in range(10000)])
```

### Type

- [ ] Bugfix
- [x] Enhancement

### Description of the changes
This consolidates some stuff in `Cleanup`

The primary things here are:
- The code that asks the user if they still want to continue when attempting to delete more than 100 messages is now in its own function that returns True if they want to continue and False if not
- The code to get the list of messages to be deleted is now in its own function, which takes the command's context, the channel and the number of messages to get for deletion as required parameters, and a check (defaulting to `lambda x: True`, which is good for functions where we don't care about checking something about the message) as an optional parameter used by the function itself. The other params (limit, before, and after) are all passed to `channel.history`, and all default to the documented default values for those params.

These functions could also probably be used by third-party cogs, so if we'd rather move these to `utils` somewhere, we could

This will need testing though to ensure that all `cleanup` commands still function as intended after these changes

#276 the id__in was provided as a list and requests handles that as 2 separate query k=v pairs

 * see: http://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#passing-parameters-in-urls
 * a `k=[v1, v2]` is passed as `?k=v1&k=v2` on the query string of an url
 * we need `id__in=id1,id2` - so we join the uuids as a list with a `','.join([id1, id2])`

See #164

pr_title: Update example instructions to use yarn.

pr_body:
This adds cli commands similar to `datacube dataset` sub-commands, but operate explicitly on file-based locations.

```
 $ datacube file --help
Usage: datacube file [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

  Manage file-based dataset locations

Options:
  -h, --help  Show this message and exit.

Commands:
  archive  Archive file locations
  info     Display known datasets at location
  restore  Restore file locations
```

```
 $ datacube file info --help
Usage: datacube file info [OPTIONS] [FILES]...

  Display known datasets at location

Options:
  --show-sources       Also show source datasets
  --show-derived       Also show derived datasets
  -f [csv|yaml]        Output format  [default: yaml]
  --max-depth INTEGER  Maximum sources/derived depth to travel
  -h, --help           Show this message and exit.
```

```
 $ datacube file archive --help
Usage: datacube file archive [OPTIONS] [FILES]...

  Archive file locations

Options:
  --dry-run   Don't archive. Display datasets that would get archived
  -h, --help  Show this message and exit.
```

```
 $ datacube file restore --help
Usage: datacube file restore [OPTIONS] [FILES]...

  Restore file locations

Options:
  --dry-run   Don't restore. Display datasets that would get restored
  -h, --help  Show this message and exit.
```

(I deliberately called the subcommand `file`, as I suspect a similar `location` or `uri` set of subcommands could be added in the future too, that take any uris. `file`was prioritised as it's immediately useful to DEA at NCI: we do these tasks all the time.)

----

There are a few other misc changes included which I don't think will be controversial, but I can split them out as needed: 
- improving field descriptions (they are shown now on the dea dashboard)
- Fix the accidental skip of a model test (a bug found by @petewa)

### Reason for this pull request
The class `DescriptorQuery` does not seem to be used anywhere. The AE/EE branch
also seems to have moved on.

### Proposed changes
- Remove `DescriptorQuery` and its helper function `_range_to_search`
- Remove associated tests

This is a fixup for 3b831cce397b13b7835bf9384cee75241ee71a73. It turns
out that warning message filters do an *anchored* match, so you have
to write a regexp that matches the beginning of the message. It only
seemed to work because the issue I was trying to fix was intermittent
to start with. Learn something every day...

Before this fix, on 3.5.0 I got a segfault once every ~2-4 runs. After
this fix, I ran 100 times and they all passed.

pr_title: resolver: when downloading a file-url fall back to url for basename, …
pr_body:
When downloading, to a known directory, a `file://` URL referencing a path within that directory, the resolver assumed the URL to be opaque and created a safe filename version of that URL. Which adds up to exponentially long filenames when repeated.

This PR catches that case (basename not set, file-URL pointing to the workspace directory) and should result in a no-op if that file already exists.

pr_title: Use a Generator when processing events.
pr_body:
#### Pull Request Checklist:
- [X] Tests for the changes have been added
- [X] Docs have been added / updated

### What kind of change does this PR introduce?
Using a Generator pattern for converting events to list of records. This is an improvement because it removes SchemaLoader and StreamingTransformerSchema from the global context that would need to be picked by spark. Since these can potentially pull in arbitrary code via custom import it was not possible to ensure that SparkRunner works correctly.
With this change the SchemaLoader and StreamingTransformerSchema objects are loaded once per partition of data in spark.


Internal - No doc update needed.

pr_title: Clean up logging and fix double writing.
pr_body:
There are some concepts, some ideas against which the world unites and
with a single voice shouts "Never Again." To this list I nominate global,
mutable variables. Against my better judgement I used a global set to deal
with python's logging global-ness. And naturally it is the source of the
first real, honest-to-God bug in production.

Now that I'm more confident about the way the context I am just going to
totally avoid python's horrific global logger registry and just create
loggers directly. This ends up being a lot easier to think about,
manage, and test.

Add/improve support for MSYS and Cygwin shells on Windows
pr_body:
Virtualenv currently copies the `ACTIVATE_SH` script on Windows when it detects it's being run from cygwin, and this patch adds support for doing the same with MSYS/MinGW as well. The only trick is that `os.abspath` doesn't produce paths that MSYS likes, so I've added a couple lines to translate `c:\path\to\env` to `/c/path/to/env` just for that file. 

I try to stay away from `cmd.exe` as much as possible, so I've been using a patched version of virtualenv on my own installation of MSYS for a few weeks and everything has worked perfectly.

**Update**: Despite what I said above, @cliffxuan has pointed out that virtualenv actually *doesn't* treat Cygwin specially. since the `OSTYPE` trick doesn't work. So, I've expanded the scope of this branch slightly to include some fixes to help on that front as well.

pr_title: treat None values as empty string (instead of 'None')
pr_body:
in our application the backend (sqlalchemy) returns None values, which by default are rendered as 'None' strings in the form. We had to manually convert them to empty strings when (de-) serializing. instead of either a) creating our own String type or b) manually converting them in all our views, we tried adding this treatment directly to colander.

the question is: does serializing the None value to the string 'None' ever make sense in the context colander is used? (it makes perfect sense for pythons `unicode(None)` to return `'None'`, of course).

what do you think? does the change we made make sense?
